subject: Welcome to FictOS Sandbox!
body:
Hi Learner,

Welcome to your FictOS Sandbox environment!

This is a fully browser-based Linux desktop. Nothing is saved to our servers — all your files and changes live in this browser tab's memory and are preserved across page refreshes via sessionStorage.

Here's what you can do right now:

  • Open the Terminal and run shell commands (ls, cd, cat, nano, git, ...)
  • Create and edit files with the Text Editor
  • Look up documentation in the Browser (MDN, Wikipedia are pre-approved)
  • Compose and send messages in Email (fictional — nothing is sent for real)

To get started, check out the exercises in ~/exercises/.

Happy learning!

— The IT Learning Hub Team

subject: Quick tip: git is available in the terminal
body:
Hi,

Just a quick note: the terminal includes a full git implementation.

Try it:

  cd ~/exercises/03-git
  git init
  echo '# My repo' > README.md
  git add README.md
  git commit -m 'Initial commit'
  git log

All the standard git commands work: init, add, commit, log, status, diff, branch, checkout, merge, and more.

The git state is stored entirely in the virtual filesystem, so it persists across page reloads just like your files.

Good luck!

— Curriculum Team
